standards.label: Documentation Quality
standards.tagline: Is the project understandable to humans?

checks.label: Usage / examples
checks.detail: Looks for code blocks or usage.

standards.label: llms.txt
standards.tagline: Can AI agents discover your docs?

checks.label: llms.txt present
checks.fix: Add an llms.txt to guide AI agents (llmstxt.org).

checks.label: Contains structured links
checks.detail: Markdown links for agents to follow.
checks.fix: List key docs as markdown links in llms.txt.

checks.label: AGENTS.md present
checks.fix: Add an AGENTS.md describing build/test commands for agents.

standards.label: Web & SEO Readiness
standards.tagline: Is the app discoverable and deployable?

standards.label: Inline API Docs
standards.tagline: Are exported APIs documented in code?

findings.title: Possible SQL injection
findings.detail: Query appears to interpolate user input directly into SQL. Use parameterized queries.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements instead of string concatenation.

findings.title: Possible SQL injection
findings.detail: Query appears to interpolate user input directly into SQL. Use parameterized queries.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements instead of string concatenation.

findings.title: Possible SQL injection
findings.detail: Query appears to interpolate user input directly into SQL. Use parameterized queries.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements instead of string concatenation.

findings.title: Possible SQL injection
findings.detail: Query appears to interpolate user input directly into SQL. Use parameterized queries.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements instead of string concatenation.

findings.title: Possible SQL injection
findings.detail: Query appears to interpolate user input directly into SQL. Use parameterized queries.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements instead of string concatenation.

findings.title: MongoDB connection without TLS
findings.detail: The connection on MongoDB does not enforce SSL/TLS. Traffic to unknown may be sent in clear text.
findings.recommendation: Append sslmode=require (Postgres/MySQL) or use a TLS connection string.

findings.title: No connection pooling detected
findings.detail: Direct Postgres connections without pooling can exhaust connections under load on serverless.
findings.recommendation: Use a pooled driver (Neon serverless, @vercel/postgres) or a pooler like PgBouncer.

findings.title: No Mongoose schema or JSON Schema validator found
findings.detail: MongoDB is in use but no Mongoose models or collection validators were detected, so document shapes are unconstrained.
findings.recommendation: Define Mongoose schemas (or $jsonSchema validators) to enforce document structure.

findings.title: Mutation without auth check
findings.detail: This POST handler changes state but no session/user lookup was detected.
findings.recommendation: Verify the caller's session before mutating data, or confirm the route is intentionally public.

findings.title: Request body not validated
findings.detail: The handler reads request input but no schema validation (zod/yup/valibot) was found.
findings.recommendation: Validate and parse the request body with a schema before using it.

findings.detail: No try/catch or .catch() guards this handler's I/O, so failures may surface as unhandled 500s.
findings.recommendation: Wrap I/O in try/catch and return a structured error response.
